questions.explanation: Images are naturally represented as dense numeric tensors; NumPy ndarrays efficiently store and operate on HxWxC arrays.
questions.question: In Python, which data structure is most appropriate to store an image as a height x width x channels array for computer vision processing?

questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows prior to grouping/aggregation; HAVING filters after aggregation.
questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows before aggregation (e.g., before a GROUP BY) when selecting training metadata for a vision dataset?

questions.explanation: Normalizing/scaling pixel intensities helps standardize brightness/contrast variations and stabilizes training.

options.A: Per-channel normalization (mean/std) or scaling pixel values

questions.question: Which preprocessing step is most directly used to reduce sensitivity to lighting changes in images?

questions.explanation: F1-score balances precision and recall, making it more robust than accuracy when classes are imbalanced.
questions.question: For a binary image classification problem with strong class imbalance, which metric is generally more informative than accuracy?

questions.explanation: Pooling reduces spatial dimensions, lowers computation, and can improve robustness to small translations.

options.A: Downsample feature maps to reduce spatial resolution and add some translation invariance
options.B: Increase the number of channels to expand model capacity

questions.question: In a CNN, what is the primary purpose of a pooling layer (e.g., max pooling) in many vision architectures?

questions.explanation: Passing training=False (or using model.predict) runs layers like Dropout/BatchNorm in inference behavior.

options.B: Call tf.GradientTape() around the forward pass
options.C: Set model.trainable = False and always call model(x, training=True)
options.D: Compile the model with a different optimizer

questions.question: In TensorFlow/Keras, which approach correctly ensures the model runs in inference mode (e.g., disables dropout) when generating predictions?

questions.explanation: torch.no_grad() disables autograd tracking, which is standard for evaluation/inference.
questions.question: In PyTorch, which snippet correctly prevents gradient tracking during evaluation to reduce memory usage?

questions.explanation: mAP summarizes precision-recall tradeoffs across confidence thresholds (and often IoU thresholds), handling images with no objects via standard PR computation.

options.A: Compute mean Average Precision (mAP) using precision-recall curves over multiple IoU thresholds
options.B: Report only training loss at the final epoch
options.C: Use accuracy on whether any box was predicted in an image
options.D: Use MSE between predicted and true box coordinates without matching

questions.question: You are evaluating an object detector on a dataset where some images have no instances of a target class. Which evaluation approach best reflects real-world performance across confidence thresholds?

questions.explanation: Canary monitoring with guardrails should trigger rollback and alerting when key metrics regress beyond thresholds.

options.A: Automatically roll back to the previous model version and alert, based on a predefined performance regression threshold
options.B: Promote the model to 100% traffic because tests passed
options.C: Disable monitoring to avoid noisy alerts
options.D: Increase the learning rate and redeploy immediately

questions.question: You are deploying a vision model with CI/CD. A new model version passes unit tests but causes a 3% drop in production F1 on a canary slice. What is the most appropriate automated action in a robust MLOps pipeline?

questions.explanation: Tiled inference with overlap reduces boundary effects and better aligns inference with crop-based training, improving segmentation quality on large images.

options.A: Use sliding-window/tiling inference with overlap and blending (or test-time augmentation) to match training distribution and reduce edge artifacts
options.B: Remove all normalization layers from the network
options.C: Switch the loss to MSE without changing inference
options.D: Convert the model to FP64 to improve numerical precision

questions.question: A segmentation model trained with random crops performs poorly on full-resolution images at inference due to border artifacts and scale mismatch. Which solution is most appropriate for production inference?
